title: Hieracium coazii
taxonomy: Hieracium coazii is classified within the family Asteraceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It belongs to the subfamily Cichorioideae, which includes plants such as dandelions and lettuce. At the genus level, Hieracium coazii is a member of the genus Hieracium, a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. The species Hieracium coazii is further divided into several subspecies, each with distinct characteristics. Within this classification, Hieracium coazii is considered a rare and endemic species.

intro: Hieracium coazii, also known as Coaz's Hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is believed to offer health benefits in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium coazii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Hieracium coazii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments, to take advantage of its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory effects.
